case_results.query_text: What does the FWC system do?
case_results.expected_relevant_passage: The FWC system is designed to collect food waste from attached macerator stations using vacuum generated by the integrated pump and transfer the macerated slurry to the food waste dewatering press or holding tank.
case_results.notes: Tests semantic retrieval over descriptive text.

case_results.query_text: What are the maintenance intervals for the macerator?
case_results.expected_relevant_passage: Cleaning after daily use; check line strainer first after a month then when needed; preventive maintenance 1 first after 1 month then after 1 year and 3 yearly; preventive maintenance 2 first at 2 years and 3 yearly; preventive maintenance 3 first at 3 years and 3 yearly; wear replacement after approx. 9000 operating hours.

case_results.query_text: What should I do if the disposer reduces speed, stops, or does not start?
case_results.expected_section_path_text: 7 Components > 7.1 Macerators > Trouble Shooting > Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start
expected_section_paths: Disposer Reduces Speed, Stops or does not Start
case_results.expected_relevant_passage: Press the red stop button, isolate and lock out power, use protective gloves, open the inlet lid, check for a jam, use the jam release wrench to rotate the grinding disc until it turns freely, remove non-grindable objects, close lid, reset breakers/overload, and restart.
